docs.title: 🚀 Build project
docs.text:
Install 1/1: Setup JDK 21.

sdk use java 21.0.2-amzn

Install ½: Install the dependencies in your project.

mvn clean install

docs.title: 🚀 Deploy with K8s
docs.text: Use the infra repository blank-infra to deploy with K8s

docs.title: Start with infrastructure
docs.text:
😀 To start the Kafka Cluster and Postgres.

make docker-up

⛔️ To the Kafka Cluster and Postgres stop or destroy: 

make docker-down

docs.title: Run APP
docs.text:
😀 To start the blank Service.

make run-app

docs.title: Logger & ELK
docs.text:
This project is prepared to send log files and process visualization with filebeat. You can specify the directory for stored the *.log files. Now, genera two file logs.

Simple log * [log.path]/[application_name]-simple.log

Complex log * [log.path]/[application_name]-complex.log

  • Specify the directory with log.path property.

Simple: Simple details about application logs. Complex: More details about application logs.

docs.title: Data Access Module
docs.text:
Part I.

  • Secondary Adapter to DB
  • Mapper
  • Explicit Repositories to DB
flowchart TB
   subgraph adapter
   end
   subgraph entity

   end
   subgraph exception

   end
   subgraph mapper
   end
   subgraph repository
   end

   repository --> adapter
   mapper --> adapter
   entity --> adapter
   exception --> adapter

docs.title: Follow these steps
docs.text:
1. Create an entity class.
  2. [Optional] Create an exception class.
  3. Create a mapper class:
    1. Method From Entity to Domain Model.
    2. Method From Domain Model to Entity.
  4. Create a Jpa Repository.
  5. Create an adapter for jpa repository (Secondary Adapter).
    1. [Optional] Implement Domain OutPort(Repository).

docs.title: Defined Primary Adapters
docs.text:
Allows the isolated domain to communicate with an outside. Orchestrate transaction, security, looking up proper aggregates and saving state changes of the domain to the database. Does not common any business logic. Domain event listeners are special kind of Application services that are triggered by domain events. Each domain event listener can have a separate domain service to handle business logic.

Part I. - DTOs - Mappers - Exceptions - Ports - Input - Listeners - Domain Service - Output - Publishers - Repositories

Part II. - implementing input ports

docs.title: Why use Lombok?
docs.text: Lombok is a Java library that helps to reduce boilerplate code in Java classes, such as constructors, getters, and setters. While it can be useful, some developers prefer not to use it due to concerns about code readability and maintainability.

docs.title: Why ?
docs.text: YES: In others modules: e.g: like in the Infrastructure module will use lombook to prevent Bolirplates Java codes.

docs.title: Business Logic
docs.text:
Business logic that cannot fit in the aggregate. Used when multiple aggregates required in business logic Can interact with other domain services

Use or need of common domain module

  • BaseEntity
  • AggregateRoot
  • BaseId(e.g: V.O)

Part I.

  • Aggregate Root
  • Entities
  • Value Object

Part II

  • Exception classes
  • Domain Events
  • Domain Service

docs.title: Why not use Lombok
docs.text: Lombok is a Java library that helps to reduce boilerplate code in Java classes, such as constructors, getters, and setters. While it can be useful, some developers prefer not to use it due to concerns about code readability and maintainability.

docs.title: Why no?
docs.text: NOT: For the core logic, is not recommended.It is bad idea use any framework that autogenare code, becasue, you need to see code directly, maybe add any validation.

docs.title: 🕹️ Plugins to auto-generate builder without lose the control
docs.text: InnerBuilder

docs.title: How do you implement a domain service?
docs.text:
Starter Describe step per step to implement a blank domain service.

⏳ Analyse the context and begin by Acceptance Test and Domain Core...

docs.title: Acceptance Test Module
Domain Core Module
Application Service Domain Module
Data Access Module
Message Module
External Module
API Module
Container Module
Calling External System
docs.text:
Rest, Soap, gRpc, GraphQL, IA strategy, etc.

First, You must create or have an output port in the application service layer. So, You create an adapter for implement these ports.

flowchart LR
   subgraph application service
       output_port
   end
   subgraph external module
     secondary_adapter
   end
    output_port --> secondary_adapter

Part I.

  • DTO to client specific.
  • Mappers

Part II.

  • Implementing output ports(Publishers/Producers)
    • Secondary Adapter

docs.title: Dependencies
Publisher
docs.text:
First, You must create or have an output port in the application service layer. So, You create an adapter for implement these port.

flowchart LR
   subgraph application service
       output_port
   end
   subgraph messaging module
     secondary_adapter
   end
    output_port --> secondary_adapter

Part I.

  • Models from Event Specification(e.g: Avro Models)
  • Mappers

Part II.

  • Implementing output ports(Publishers/Producers)
    • Secondary Adapter
  • Implementing input ports(Listener/Consumers)
    • Primary Adapter
